name: Pryderi Extended
description: Pryderi, son of Pwyll and Rhiannon, is a significant figure in Welsh mythology, particularly in the Mabinogion. Born after Rhiannon's unjust accusation of infanticide, Pryderi disappears shortly after birth, only to be found later by Teyrnon Twryf Liant, who raises him as his own. Pryderi eventually returns to his true parents, restoring Rhiannon's honor and solidifying his place as a prince of Dyfed. He later inherits the seven cantrefs of Dyfed and the three of Ystrad Clud from Manawydan, becoming a powerful ruler. He marries Cigfa and is known for his bravery and wisdom. Pryderi's involvement in the tale of Math fab Mathonwy leads to his tragic death at the hands of Gwydion, highlighting the dangers of magic and deceit. His life embodies themes of loss, redemption, and the cyclical nature of fate, making him a compelling and enduring character in Welsh lore. Pryderi represents the ideal of a just and capable ruler, and his story serves as a cautionary tale about the consequences of pride and unchecked ambition. His experiences with otherworldly forces and his eventual sacrifice contribute to the rich tapestry of Welsh mythology, showcasing the interplay between the mortal and supernatural realms. He is an important figure in the Four Branches of the Mabinogi, linking the stories of Pwyll, Branwen, Manawydan, and Math.
subtitle: Prince of Dyfed, Son of Pwyll
achievements: Being raised by Teyrnon Twryf Liant
Participating in the war against Gwynedd

keyMyths.title: The Birth and Disappearance
keyMyths.description: Pryderi is born but disappears mysteriously, leading to Rhiannon's false accusation. He is later found and raised by Teyrnon Twryf Liant before returning to his parents.

keyMyths.title: Inheritance of Dyfed
keyMyths.description: Pryderi inherits the lands of Dyfed and Ystrad Clud from Manawydan, becoming a powerful ruler. This marks him as a significant figure in the region.

keyMyths.title: Death at the Hands of Gwydion
keyMyths.description: Pryderi is tricked and killed by Gwydion in the tale of Math fab Mathonwy. This highlights the dangers of magic and the consequences of pride.

sources.author: Gwyn Jones and Thomas Jones
sources.description: A translation of the Mabinogion tales.
sources.text: A translation of the Mabinogion tales.

sources.author: John Matthews
